task: Write a Python function collapse_delims(s) that splits the string on commas, treats any run of consecutive commas as one split, drops empty pieces at the start and end, and returns the pieces rejoined with a single comma between each. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided function collapses consecutive delimiters, removes leading/trailing empty pieces, and rejoins the remaining pieces with commas.
trace: First, I replaced all consecutive commas with a single comma to eliminate multiple splits in the string. Next, I trimmed any leading or trailing commas from the string. Lastly, I split the cleaned string by commas and rejoined the pieces with a single comma between each. To handle an edge case where there could be multiple consecutive commas at the start or end of the string, I first replaced all such instances before trimming.
